analysis.executive_summary: Customers consistently single out installation quality and honest pricing — the two themes behind this month’s 4.7-star average. The pattern to watch is scheduling: spring-rush wait times generated every piece of critical feedback this month, and review responses, while improving, still trail the 80% benchmark for top-rated home-service businesses.

insights.top_strength: Installation quality is your reputation engine: 19 of 38 written reviews this month praise the crew’s workmanship, punctuality, or cleanliness.
insights.biggest_concern: Scheduling friction during the spring rush — every sub-4-star review this month mentions wait times or unreturned calls.
insights.competitive_advantage: You are the only top-ranked installer in the area whose reviews repeatedly mention rebate paperwork being handled for the customer — a differentiator worth promoting.
insights.improvement_opportunity: Lifting review responses from 63% to the 80% benchmark is the fastest available gain — unanswered reviews are concentrated on Yelp and Facebook.
insights.benchmark_note: Your 4.7★ average sits above the 4.3★ median for HVAC contractors in Boulder County.
insights.response_priority: Respond first to the 17 unanswered reviews from the last 90 days, starting with the two that mention scheduling.

positive.sample_quote: The crew left the utility room cleaner than they found it.
The final invoice matched the estimate to the dollar.
They walked us through the rebate paperwork.

negative.sample_quote: Getting on the calendar took almost three weeks.

categories.name: Installation quality
categories.action_note: Keep publishing before/after photos of installs — reviewers reference them.
categories.sample_quote: House held 70° through the last cold snap.

categories.name: Pricing & value
categories.action_note: Quote-to-invoice accuracy is being noticed; make the guarantee explicit on the website.

categories.name: Communication
categories.action_note: Confirmation calls are falling through during the rush; add an automated booking confirmation.
categories.sample_quote: I had to call twice to confirm the date.

categories.name: Scheduling & timeliness
categories.action_note: Publish realistic lead times on the booking page so expectations are set before the call.
categories.sample_quote: Three weeks to get on the calendar.

quick_wins.action: Reply to the 17 unanswered reviews from the last 90 days — start with the two that mention scheduling.
Add neighborhood names (North Boulder, Gunbarrel, Louisville) to your Google Business Profile service descriptions.
Upload photos from the last five installs to your profile — reviews that mention photos convert better.

action_tracking.previous_recommendation: Respond to every new review within 48 hours
action_tracking.verdict: Response rate climbed from 50% to 63% — keep pushing toward the 80% benchmark.
action_tracking.evidence: 29 of 46 May reviews received a response, up from 21 of 42 in April.

action_tracking.previous_recommendation: Ask recent install customers for a Google review
action_tracking.verdict: Done — review volume grew 9.5% month over month, almost entirely on Google.
action_tracking.evidence: 46 new reviews in May vs 42 in April.

immediate.title: Close the review-response gap
immediate.action: Respond to the 17 unanswered reviews, then hold the 48-hour response rule through the summer rush.
immediate.description: Response rate is 63% against an 80% benchmark; unanswered reviews cluster on Yelp and Facebook.
immediate.impact: Directly lifts the response component of your review score (currently 63/100).

short_term.title: Fix peak-season scheduling communication
short_term.action: Add automated booking confirmations and publish current lead times on the booking page.
short_term.description: Every critical review this month traces back to scheduling friction, not workmanship.
short_term.impact: Removes the single source of negative feedback; protects the 4.7★ average into the busy season.

long_term.title: Turn the maintenance plan into a review engine
long_term.action: Add a review ask to the post-visit follow-up for maintenance-plan customers (2 visits/year each).
long_term.description: Plan customers are your happiest segment but rarely review twice; a seasonal touchpoint changes that.
long_term.impact: A steady source of fresh reviews — recency is a local ranking factor.

source_analysis.notable: Google drives 83% of your review volume — and is the only source feeding your map rankings.
source_analysis.action: Keep the post-install review ask focused on Google.

source_analysis.notable: Both scheduling complaints this month landed on Yelp; neither has a response yet.
source_analysis.action: Respond to the two open Yelp reviews this week.

source_analysis.notable: Low volume but high intent — reviewers reference being repeat customers.
source_analysis.action: Cross-post install photos here; it costs nothing.

temporal_patterns.seasonal_note: Review volume tracks install season; expect the June–August peak to add 50+ reviews.

highlights.author: Sarah Mitchell
highlights.message: Bluebird replaced our 20-year-old furnace with a cold-climate heat pump and the difference is night and day. The crew showed up on time, walked us through the rebate paperwork, and left the utility room cleaner than they found it. House held 70° through the last cold snap.
highlights.reply_message: Thank you Sarah! Enjoy the new system, and remember the first maintenance visit is on us next spring.

highlights.author: James Okafor
highlights.message: From quote to finished installation in nine days. Transparent pricing — the final invoice matched the estimate to the dollar — and the technician took the time to set up the smart thermostat schedules with us before leaving.
highlights.reply_message: Thanks James, glad the schedule worked out. Call us any time.

highlights.author: Dana Whitfield
highlights.title: Great work, slow to schedule
highlights.message: The installation itself was excellent and the price was fair. Only complaint is that getting on the calendar took almost three weeks in the spring rush, and I had to call twice to confirm the date.

highlights.author: Robert Chen
highlights.message: Second year on their maintenance plan. They call me to schedule the tune-up before I remember it myself, and the AC has never run better. Honest company — they told me a repair could wait instead of selling me a replacement.
highlights.reply_message: Appreciate the trust, Robert — see you in the fall for the furnace check.

recommendations.title: Low top-3 visibility
recommendations.description: A 10.2% visibility score means most nearby searches do not surface you in the top 3 map results — the positions that capture the large majority of calls. The fastest levers: review velocity, keyword-rich service descriptions, and fresh photos.

recommendations.title: Positive momentum
recommendations.description: Average map ranking has improved 18.4% since tracking began in December 2025 (position 10.3 → 8.4). The work is compounding — hold the course.

recommendations.title: Weak area: north of downtown
recommendations.description: Rankings are weakest in the northern third of the service area (average position 10.3). Targeting North Boulder and Gunbarrel by name in service pages and GBP posts would close the gap.
